task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

input.content:
NovaBridge Project Health Briefing — Project Helios
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: 2026-07-11

Executive Summary
Project Helios is currently ON TRACK with minor but material delivery risks. Core platform progress is strong: the API gateway is complete, the data ingestion module is nearing completion, and the client dashboard is in active build. However, several downstream commitments are compressed due to overdue integration/UAT readiness items, Meridian-side dependencies, unresolved licensing, and performance validation work that has not yet started.

The most important near-term leadership concern is the July 25 Meridian product demo, which must include live data ingestion using real Meridian sample data. Failure to resolve the Meridian data format issue, GeoSync licensing, and dashboard performance questions before the demo could affect client confidence and Q3 schedule integrity.

Current Progress
Overall status: On track, with minor risks

Milestone progress:
- API gateway: 100% complete and delivered.
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete; target completion date is 2026-07-18.
- Client dashboard: 60% complete; target completion date is 2026-07-22.
- Integration testing: 40% complete; integration test sign-off target is 2026-07-25.

Completed Q2 deliverables:
- Requirements documentation v1.0.
- System architecture design.
- API gateway implementation.
- Development environment setup.
- Data schema finalization.

In-progress / overdue deliverables:
- Data ingestion module: in progress, target 2026-07-18.
- Client dashboard v1: in progress, target 2026-07-22.
- Integration test suite setup: overdue from 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20.
- UAT environment provisioning: overdue from 2026-07-01; currently blocked by Meridian IT access, with Meridian now expected to provision access by 2026-07-19.
- Performance benchmarking report: overdue from 2026-07-05; owner is Derek Okafor.

Recent client alignment:
- Product demo date confirmed for 2026-07-25.
- Meridian will provide a clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock the date format issue.
- UAT has been extended by one week and is now scheduled for August 17-28.
- Meridian IT infrastructure diagram is due from Meridian by 2026-07-12.

Key Risks
1. Meridian source data quality issues
- Likelihood: High
- Impact: High
- Status: Active
- Current issue: Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats, including ISO 8601 and MM/DD/YYYY.
- Mitigation: Meridian has committed to providing a clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.
- Leadership relevance: This is the highest-scoring active risk and directly affects the live ingestion requirement for the July 25 demo.

2. GeoSync license renewal before demo
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: High
- Status: Active
- Current issue: Third-party mapping library GeoSync v2.1 license renewal is pending legal review.
- Target resolution: 2026-07-14.
- Leadership relevance: If not resolved before the demo, dashboard mapping functionality and demo readiness may be impaired.

3. Dashboard performance on large datasets
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: High
- Status: Active
- Current issue: Benchmark task HEL-247 has not yet started; performance benchmarking report is overdue.
- Q3 target: Dashboard load time under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Leadership relevance: Meridian explicitly raised concern about dashboard load times, and Derek Okafor is expected to benchmark before the demo.

4. UAT timeline slippage
- Likelihood: High
- Impact: Medium
- Status: Active
- Current issue: A one-week extension has already been consumed; UAT is now planned for August 17-28.
- Leadership relevance: UAT slippage greater than two weeks could push the October 1 launch into Q4 and trigger a Section 7.1 penalty review.

5. Meridian IT environment access delays
- Likelihood: Medium
- Impact: Medium
- Status: Active
- Current issue: UAT environment provisioning has been delayed by Meridian IT access dependencies.
- Current dependency: Meridian IT infrastructure diagram due 2026-07-12; UAT environment access expected by 2026-07-19.
- Leadership relevance: Any further delay will compress integration testing, UAT preparation, and launch readiness.

6. Key person dependency
- Likelihood: Low
- Impact: High
- Status: Monitored
- Current issue: Derek Okafor owns or is central to several critical items, including performance benchmarking and GeoSync resolution, with no backup technical lead identified.
- Leadership relevance: This creates delivery resilience risk during a critical Q3 window.

Upcoming Q3 Commitments
July commitments:
- 2026-07-12: Meridian to provide IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup.
- 2026-07-14: Resolve GeoSync license renewal before demo.
- 2026-07-15: Meridian to provide clean sample dataset to address data format blocker.
- 2026-07-18: Finalize data ingestion module.
- 2026-07-18: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian.
- 2026-07-19: Meridian IT to provision UAT environment access.
- 2026-07-20: Complete integration test suite setup.
- 2026-07-22: Complete client dashboard v1.
- 2026-07-25: Complete integration test sign-off.
- 2026-07-25: Conduct Meridian product demo, including live data ingestion, end-to-end pipeline walkthrough, and dashboard visualization with real data.

August commitments:
- 2026-08-17 to 2026-08-28: Meridian user acceptance testing.
- Support Meridian user testing sessions.
- Address UAT feedback, with two sprints budgeted.
- Continue performance hardening.

August-September commitments:
- Dashboard load time target: under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Ingestion throughput benchmarking target: 500k records per minute sustained.
- External security penetration test; vendor still to be determined.

September commitments:
- Production environment setup.
- Runbook documentation.
- Hypercare plan finalization for a 30-day post-launch model.
- Final client training sessions.

Launch target:
- 2026-10-01.

Potential Q3 scope additions subject to change control approval:
- Scheduled ingestion jobs using cron-based triggers.
- Email alerting for pipeline failures.
- Self-service mapping rule editor, deferred from Q2.

Issues Requiring Leadership Attention
1. Protect the July 25 demo date and scope
The demo is confirmed and must include live data ingestion rather than mock data. Leadership should ensure the delivery team has clear prioritization to resolve demo-critical blockers first: clean data availability, GeoSync licensing, dashboard v1 completion, and performance benchmarking.

2. Expedite GeoSync license resolution
The license renewal target is 2026-07-14. If legal review is delayed, leadership may need to intervene to prevent demo or delivery impact.

3. Increase visibility on performance benchmarking
The performance benchmarking report is overdue, and dashboard performance has already been raised by Meridian. Leadership should request an immediate status update and a pre-demo benchmark result, even if preliminary.

4. Monitor Meridian dependency dates closely
Meridian commitments on the IT infrastructure diagram, clean sample dataset, and UAT environment access are now on the critical path. Leadership should consider sponsor-level escalation if any of these dates slip.

5. Reduce key-person risk around Derek Okafor
Derek is associated with several high-impact workstreams during a compressed timeline. Leadership should consider assigning a backup technical lead or focused support to reduce execution risk.

6. Enforce change control for proposed Q3 feature additions
Several potential feature additions are listed for Q3 but remain subject to change control. Given the current UAT and launch sensitivity, leadership should avoid approving additional scope unless impact on timeline, cost, and penalty exposure is clearly understood.

Recommended Leadership Actions
- Confirm that demo-critical work is the top priority through July 25.
- Ask Legal for a firm GeoSync renewal decision or escalation path by 2026-07-14.
- Request a dashboard performance benchmark update before the Meridian demo.
- Track Meridian dependency dates on July 12, July 15, and July 19, with escalation ready if missed.
- Assign backup technical coverage for Derek Okafor’s critical responsibilities.
- Require formal change control review for any Q3 feature additions.

Health Assessment
- Schedule: Amber/Green — overall on track, but July commitments are tightly sequenced and vulnerable to dependency slippage.
- Scope: Amber — proposed additions should be controlled to protect launch readiness.
- Risk: Amber — data quality, licensing, performance, and UAT timing require close management.
- Client readiness: Amber — demo expectations are clear but demanding; live data requirement increases execution risk.
- Launch outlook: Green/Amber — October 1 remains achievable if July blockers are resolved and UAT does not slip by more than two weeks.
